clinical_study.brief_title: FOLFIRINOX Plus Adagrasib (KRAS G12V Inhibitor) as Neoadjuvant Therapy in Borderline Resectable PDAC (KRAS-FOLFI)
clinical_study.official_title: A Phase 2 Randomized Trial of FOLFIRINOX With or Without Adagrasib in the Neoadjuvant Treatment of KRAS G12V-Mutant Borderline Resectable Pancreatic Ductal Adenocarcinoma

brief_summary.textblock: This trial evaluates whether adding adagrasib — a selective covalent KRAS G12V inhibitor — to neoadjuvant FOLFIRINOX improves R0 resection rates and pathologic response in patients with borderline resectable pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (PDAC) harboring a KRAS G12V mutation, compared to FOLFIRINOX alone.

detailed_description.textblock: Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (PDAC) carries KRAS mutations in over 90% of cases, with KRAS G12V representing approximately 20-25% of all KRAS mutations in PDAC. Adagrasib (MRTX849) was initially developed as a KRAS G12C inhibitor but modified analogs targeting G12V have shown promising activity in preclinical PDAC models. Borderline resectable PDAC (BRPC), defined per NCCN/AHPBA criteria as tumor contact with major mesenteric vessels without occlusion or greater than 180-degree encasement of the SMA, is treated with neoadjuvant chemotherapy to improve surgical margin status. FOLFIRINOX remains the preferred neoadjuvant regimen in fit patients. This study randomizes patients with KRAS G12V-mutant BRPC to modified FOLFIRINOX x 8 cycles (4 months) + adagrasib 600 mg BID orally versus modified FOLFIRINOX x 8 cycles alone, followed by restaging and surgical evaluation. Primary endpoint is R0 resection rate (margin-negative resection by >= 1 mm). Key secondary endpoints include pathologic response rate (Evans Grade III-IV), conversion to resectability, progression-free survival, and overall survival. ctDNA dynamics are assessed as an exploratory biomarker.

primary_outcome.time_frame: At time of surgical resection, approximately 4-5 months after treatment initiation

secondary_outcome.measure: Pathologic Response Rate (Evans Grade III or IV)
secondary_outcome.time_frame: At time of surgical resection, approximately 4-5 months after treatment initiation

secondary_outcome.measure: Rate of Surgical Resection (any margin status)

secondary_outcome.time_frame: From randomization until radiographic progression or death, up to 24 months
From randomization until death from any cause, up to 36 months

secondary_outcome.measure: Radiographic Response Rate per RECIST 1.1 after neoadjuvant therapy
secondary_outcome.time_frame: After 8 cycles of neoadjuvant treatment, approximately 4 months
During treatment period, up to 5 months

criteria.textblock:
Inclusion Criteria: - Histologically or cytologically confirmed pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (PDAC); mixed histologies including acinar cell or neuroendocrine components are excluded - KRAS G12V mutation confirmed by a validated molecular assay on tumor tissue or ctDNA; patients with other KRAS mutations (G12D, G12C, G12R, etc.) are not eligible for this study (separate cohorts may be available) - Borderline resectable disease as defined by NCCN 2024 criteria: (a) Pancreatic head/uncinate process: SMA contact >= 180 degrees, or abutment of first jejunal SMA branch, or SMV/PV involvement with reconstructible occlusion, or IVC contact (b) Pancreatic body/tail: celiac axis abutment <= 180 degrees without aortic involvement - No evidence of distant metastatic disease (M0) by CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with IV contrast within 28 days of enrollment - No prior systemic chemotherapy, radiation therapy, or surgery for pancreatic cancer - Age >= 18 years - ECOG Performance Status 0-1 - Adequate bone marrow: ANC >= 1500/mm3, platelets >= 100,000/mm3, hemoglobin >= 9 g/dL - Adequate hepatic function: total bilirubin <= 1.5x ULN (< 2x ULN if biliary stent in place), AST/ALT <= 2.5x ULN - Adequate renal function: creatinine clearance >= 50 mL/min (Cockcroft-Gault) - Adequate nutritional status: albumin >= 3.0 g/dL; weight loss < 15% of body weight over 6 months - Biliary stent placement is permitted for bile duct obstruction prior to enrollment; metallic stents preferred for endoscopic biliary decompression - Peripheral neuropathy <= Grade 1 at baseline - Multidisciplinary pancreatic tumor board review confirming borderline resectable classification required before enrollment - Signed informed consent
Exclusion Criteria: - Locally advanced (unresectable) PDAC: SMA encasement > 270 degrees, celiac axis encasement, aortic invasion, or unreconstructible SMV/PV occlusion - Resectable PDAC (no vascular contact or < 180 degree SMA contact with preserved fat plane) - Metastatic disease (M1): liver metastases, peritoneal carcinomatosis, lung metastases, or any distant organ involvement - KRAS mutations other than G12V (KRAS WT, G12D, G12C, G12R); KRAS WT PDAC excluded - Prior treatment with any KRAS inhibitor - Neuroendocrine carcinoma, acinar cell carcinoma, or other non-ductal pancreatic tumors - Active biliary sepsis or uncontrolled biliary obstruction at time of enrollment - Known dihydropyrimidine dehydrogenase (DPD) deficiency - Active or severe hepatic impairment (Child-Pugh B or C) - Known hypersensitivity to any component of FOLFIRINOX or adagrasib - Concurrent use of strong CYP3A4 inhibitors or inducers - Pregnancy or breastfeeding; effective contraception required - Gilbert syndrome or UGT1A1 polymorphism (7/7 homozygous): irinotecan dose reduction required per protocol amendment; not excluded - Active second malignancy requiring systemic therapy within 3 years

intervention.description: Modified FOLFIRINOX (oxaliplatin 85 mg/m2 IV + leucovorin 400 mg/m2 IV + irinotecan 150 mg/m2 IV + 5-FU 2400 mg/m2 continuous infusion over 46 hours, without 5-FU bolus) every 2 weeks for 8 cycles, plus adagrasib 600 mg orally twice daily continuously for the duration of neoadjuvant therapy.
Modified FOLFIRINOX (oxaliplatin 85 mg/m2 IV + leucovorin 400 mg/m2 IV + irinotecan 150 mg/m2 IV + 5-FU 2400 mg/m2 continuous infusion over 46 hours, without 5-FU bolus) every 2 weeks for 8 cycles.
